title: Lyra-1 Guardrail and Content Safety

classes.name: ContentSafetyGuardrail
methods: is_safe(**kwargs) -> Tuple[bool, str]  # abstract, raises NotImplementedError

classes.name: PostprocessingGuardrail
methods: postprocess(frames: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray  # abstract, raises NotImplementedError

classes.name: GuardrailRunner
methods: run_safety_check(input: Any) -> Tuple[bool, str]  # iterates safety_models, calls is_safe(input)
postprocess(frames: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray  # iterates postprocessors, calls postprocess(frames)

classes.name: Aegis
methods: is_safe(prompt: str) -> tuple[bool, str]  # calls filter_aegis_output
classes.notes: Uses meta-llama/LlamaGuard-7b base + nvidia/Aegis-AI-Content-Safety-LlamaGuard-Defensive-1.0 LoRA adapter. Generates up to 100 tokens; checks for 'unsafe' in output.

classes.name: Blocklist
methods: check_partial_match(normalized_prompt, normalized_word, guardrail_partial_match_letter_count) -> tuple[bool, str]  # static
is_safe(input_prompt: str) -> tuple[bool, str]  # 3-stage: profanity censor, lemmatized censor, exact match
classes.notes: 3-stage pipeline: (1) profanity censor on raw input, (2) censor on lemmatized tokens, (3) whole-word/partial match against exact_match_words. Loads word lists from nvidia/Cosmos-Guardrail1/blocklist.

classes.name: LlamaGuard3
methods: is_safe(prompt: str) -> tuple[bool, str]  # calls filter_llamaGuard3_output
classes.notes: Uses meta-llama/Llama-Guard-3-8B. Applies chat template with UNSAFE_CATEGORIES. Generates up to 100 tokens; checks for 'unsafe' in output.

classes.name: VideoContentSafetyFilter
methods: __infer(pil_image: Image.Image) -> int  # encoder.encode_image → model.network → softmax → argmax
is_safe_file(filepath: str) -> bool  # reads video, samples at 2FPS, calls __infer per frame
is_safe_frames(frames: Iterable) -> bool  # iterates frames, calls __infer
classes.notes: 7 classes: Safe(0), Sexual_Content(1), Violence(2), Drugs(3), Child_Abuse(4), Hate_and_Harassment(5), Self-Harm(6). Samples video at 2 FPS; any non-safe frame → unsafe.

classes.name: SigLIPEncoder
methods: encode_image(input_img: Image.Image) -> torch.Tensor  # L2-normalized image features

classes.name: VideoSafetyModel
methods: forward(data_batch: dict[str, torch.Tensor]) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]  # returns logits

classes.name: RetinaFaceFilter
methods: preprocess_frames(frames: np.ndarray) -> torch.Tensor  # RGB→BGR, subtract mean
blur_detected_faces(frames, batch_loc, batch_conf, prior_data, scale, min_size) -> list[np.ndarray]
postprocess(frames: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray  # DataLoader batches → net inference → blur_detected_faces
classes.notes: Uses RetinaFace (ResNet50) for face detection. Applies pixelate_face() to each detected face region. Filters by confidence_threshold=0.7, NMS=0.4, TOP_K=5000, KEEP_TOP_K=750.

classes.name: VideoData
attributes: frames: np.ndarray  # Shape [B, H, W, C]

classes.name: CosmosT5TextEncoder
methods: encode_prompts(prompts: Union[str, List[str]], max_length: int = 512) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]  # returns (encoded_text [B, L, H], attn_mask [B, L])
classes.notes: Pads all prompts to max_length=512. Zero-pads encoded output beyond actual sequence length. Returns last_hidden_state.

classes.name: DummyT5TextEncoder
methods: encode_prompts(prompts, max_length=512) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]  # returns zeros tensors [B, L, 1024]
classes.notes: Testing stub; returns zero embeddings of shape (B, max_length, 1024).

factory_functions.name: run_video_guardrail
factory_functions.signature: run_video_guardrail(frames: np.ndarray, guardrail_runner: GuardrailRunner) -> np.ndarray | None

utility_classes_and_functions: VideoData (dataclass): frames: np.ndarray, fps: int, duration: int
pixelate_face(face_img, blocks=5) -> np.ndarray — cv2 resize down/up
key_patterns: Strategy pattern: GuardrailRunner accepts pluggable ContentSafetyGuardrail and PostprocessingGuardrail lists
Two-phase pipeline: (1) safety check via run_safety_check → early-exit on first failure; (2) postprocess on safe output
Text pipeline: Blocklist (rule-based) → LlamaGuard3 (LLM-based) in sequence
Video pipeline: VideoContentSafetyFilter (SigLIP+MLP classify) → RetinaFaceFilter (face blur)
Aegis is alternative LLM guardrail (LlamaGuard-7b+LoRA) vs LlamaGuard3 (8B) — same interface
CosmosT5TextEncoder is standalone (not part of GuardrailRunner); used by model pipeline for prompt encoding
important_notes: All ContentSafetyGuardrail subclasses return (bool, str): True=safe, False=blocked. GuardrailRunner short-circuits on first unsafe model. Aegis: nvidia/Aegis-AI-Content-Safety-LlamaGuard-Defensive-1.0 LoRA on LlamaGuard-7b. LlamaGuard3: meta-llama/Llama-Guard-3-8B. VideoContentSafetyFilter: SigLIP (google/siglip-so400m-patch14-384) + MLP with 7 harm classes. RetinaFaceFilter: ResNet50-based face detection with pixelation. T5TextEncoder: google-t5/t5-11b, outputs (B, 512, hidden_size) embeddings.
